summary: This source discusses Martin Heidegger's 'Being and Time', framing it not merely as abstract philosophical theory, but as an 'ethics of care' focused on the cultivation of the self. It highlights Heidegger's belief that human perfection and the realization of one's possibilities are achieved through care, while analyzing human life's inherent tendency to fall.

principles.statement: Human perfection and transformation are accomplished through care.
principles.rationale: Care is the mechanism by which a person becomes free for their ownmost possibilities and achieves their potential in Being.
principles.source_quote: man's perfection -his transformation into that which he can be in Being-free for his own most possibilities (projection) -is 'accomplished' by 'care'

mental_models.name: The Tendency to Fall
mental_models.description: A lens for analyzing human life by examining its inherent tendency to fall and its peculiar ability to face this enigma of existence.

signature_quotes.text: man's perfection -his transformation into that which he can be in Being-free for his own most possibilities (projection) -is 'accomplished' by 'care'
signature_quotes.context: Quoted by Escudero to support the view that Being and Time is a book about self-care and cultivation.

anti_patterns.description: Developing philosophical theories that ignore the fundamental question of human being and its relation to being in general.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It fails to address the constant, underlying dilemma of human existence and facticity, treating philosophy as just another abstract theory rather than a reflection on life.
